title: Fix tristate receivers not seeing immediate tristate driver value updates
body:
The following code executes wrong, when run like this: 

- `verilator --binary --timing --debug --top-module helper hdl/helper.sv ` , followed by

- `obj_dir/Vhelper` :

```
module helper
  (
   output reg dout
   );
   reg [31:0] datav;
   initial begin : ASSIGNING_Z_IS_SIGNIFICANT
      dout = 1'bz;
      for (int i = 0 ; i < 1000 ; i++) begin : MAIN_LOOP

         datav[31:0] = $urandom;
         dout = datav[31];

         if (dout != datav[31])
           $fatal (1, $sformatf("Unexpected behavior encountered. %1d %08x", dout, datav));

      end
   end
endmodule
```

What I expect to happen: The code runs silently.
What happens: The text "Unexpected behavior encoutered" goes to console.

Looking at compiled C code, it seems as if the assignment was optimized away incorrectly, and a different random values were given to `dout`and `datav`. Bug hides itself if I do not initialize dout to `z`, or do not make it a module output.

Tested with these combinations:

Verilator 5.036 2025-04-27 rev v5.036 over Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.10 (Ootpa)
Verilator 5.039 devel rev UNKNOWN.rev over Debian GNU/Linux 13 (trixie)

The latter is a blind compile of the latest commit in master branch on July 31st.
